apache.finding: Apache emits Phase 4 intervention logs and deny-status evidence before commit; current matrix does not prove a connection abort.
apache.recommended_next_step: Add or run a strict after-commit Apache evidence case before setting phase4-hard-abort as supported.

haproxy.finding: HAProxy response-body decisions are logged, but current SPOA evidence shows HTTP status decisions rather than transport aborts.
haproxy.recommended_next_step: Do not promote to PASS on hard-abort semantics unless HAProxy can produce real close/abort evidence.

nginx.finding: HAProxy response-body decisions are logged, but current SPOA evidence shows HTTP status decisions rather than transport aborts.
nginx.recommended_next_step: Do not promote to PASS on hard-abort semantics unless HAProxy can produce real close/abort evidence.

apache: connectors/apache/src/msc_filters.c emits phase4_intervention logs and can return APR_ECONNABORTED when response data was already committed.
Current harness evidence is primarily mode=safe deny_status before commit, not a runtime proof of hard abort.
haproxy: Current HAProxy/SPOA evidence includes Phase 4 response-body decisions and 403 status decisions.
No current matrix row shows transport-level connection_abort evidence for HAProxy Phase 4 response-body matches.
nginx: connectors/nginx/src/ngx_http_modsecurity_body_filter.c reports actual_action=connection_abort in strict mode after headers are sent.
